10 Tips for Better Sleep

Sleep is one of the most important pillars of health. Poor sleep affects memory, mood, and long-term well-being.

First, establish a consistent bedtime routine. Going to bed and waking up at the same time every day helps regulate your circadian rhythm.

Second, limit screen time before bed. The blue light emitted by phones and computers suppresses melatonin production.

Third, avoid caffeine after 2 PM. Even small amounts can disrupt sleep architecture.
